overview.short: Valliyoorkkavu Shree Bhagavathy Temple is an ancient Bhagavathy temple located near Mananthavady in the Wayanad district.
overview.description: Valliyoorkkavu Shree Bhagavathy Temple is an ancient Bhagavathy temple located near Mananthavady in the Wayanad district. The temple is for Goddess Bhadrakali (a form of supreme mother Adi Parashakthi) as its presiding deity. She is also worshipped in three forms namely: Bhagavathy, Vana Durga, and Jala Durga. The idol of the temple is believed to be self-manifested and the annual festival falls in the month of March and lasts for 14 days.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Payyampally
topPlaces.description: Payyampally is a town in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Oorpally
topPlaces.description: Oorpally is a village near Koyileri, Mananthavady on the banks of the river Kabini in the Wayanad district of Kerala state, south India.

topPlaces.name: Pazhassi Raja
topPlaces.description: Kerala Varma Pazhassi Raja (IPA: [pɐɻɐʃːi ɾaːd͡ʒɐ]) (3 January 1753 – 30 November 1805), also known as Cotiote Rajah and Pychy Rajah, was the de facto head of the Kottayam Kingdom in the Malabar region of Kerala between 1774 and 1805. His struggle against the British East India Company is known as the Cotiote War, for which he has been touted as one of the earliest freedom fighters in India.

topPlaces.name: Thrissilery
topPlaces.description: Thrissilery is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mananthavady
topPlaces.description: Mananthavady is a Municipality, Taluk and town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. The municipal boundaries are defined by Tirunelli Panchayat to the north, Kabini River to the east and south, and Thavinhal Panchayat to the west.

topPlaces.name: Thrissilery Shiva Temple
topPlaces.description: Thrissilery Shiva Temple is a Shiva temple situated at Thrissilery neighbourhood in Wayanad, Kerala, India. This ancient temple is dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Kattikkulam
topPlaces.description: Kattikkulam (Kartikulam) is a small town near Mananthavady in Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The office of the Thirunelly Gramapanchayath is located at Kartikulam.

topPlaces.name: Neervaram
topPlaces.description: Neervaram is a village near Panamaram in Wayanad district of Kerala state, India.

His struggle against the British East India Company is known as the Cotiote War, for which he has been touted as one of the earliest freedom fighters in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valliyoorkkavu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valliyoorkkavu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (75 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valliyoorkkavu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valliyoorkkavu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valliyoorkkavu?
faq.a: Stays in Valliyoorkkavu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valliyoorkkavu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Payyampally, Oorpally, Pazhassi Raja, Thrissilery, Mananthavady.
